value.found_quote: Pause before side effects like cancellations, edits, shell commands, or sensitive MCP actions | Human-in-the-loop approvals
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes "Human-in-the-loop approvals" as a control mechanism that pauses agent runs before sensitive tool actions (including sensitive MCP actions) so a person or policy can approve or reject them. The page includes a dedicated "Pause for human review" section with code examples showing `needsApproval: true` on tools and an interruption/resumption pattern. The table entry directly matches the claim's description of pausing before sensitive actions including MCP operations. While the page does not use the phrase "dedicated human-approval node for MCP tool operations," it does explicitly call out "sensitive MCP actions" as a use case for human-in-the-loop approvals, and the same interruption pattern applies throughout the workflow including nested calls.
